pi_items.source: Confirmed via setup_opentoonz.sh and setup_task.sh which download the official opentoonz_sample.zip from GitHub and place dwanko_run.tnz at this location.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which explicitly creates this required output directory.
Task instruction explicitly requests 'DOUBLE SPEED (2x faster)', matching this multiplier.
Matches verifier.py logic threshold. Even at half length (36 frames), a 1920x1080 sequence will vastly exceed 100KB.
Matches verifier.py parameters for calculating acceptable frame count reduction.
Matches verifier.py parameters for calculating acceptable frame count reduction.

privileged_info_summary: The source scene 'dwanko_run.tnz' is an official sample animation downloaded from the OpenToonz GitHub repository. The true length of this scene is 72 frames. However, the benchmark's `setup_task.sh` uses a Python XML parser that fails due to an unescaped ampersand ('&') in the 'Compression Type' property ('Lempel-Ziv & Welch encoding') of the TNZ file. Because of this parsing crash, the benchmark's original frame count silently defaults to 13. If an agent correctly halves the actual 72-frame animation and outputs 36 frames, `verifier.py` will incorrectly evaluate it as a failure, as 36 frames is greater than the flawed baseline of 13 frames.
